summary: SSR migration verified end-to-end. All 13 required public routes now serve full HTML (title, h1, meta description, canonical, JSON-LD, internal links) WITHOUT executing JavaScript — Googlebot/curl gets real content. React SPA still mounts cleanly on top and hides the SSR layer to avoid duplicate content. Admin auth + admin pages work via SPA fallthrough. Backend regression 23/23 pass. New SSR suite 17/17 pass.
critical_code_review_comments: ssr-server.js is 695 lines — still readable but approaching the 700-line guidance; consider splitting route renderers into a /routes folder for future maintainability.

ssr-data.js duplicates SERVICES/LOCATIONS/FAQS/NAV/BRAND from src/data/site.js. Any future content edit must be made in BOTH files or risk SSR drifting from SPA. Suggest a build step that imports from a single source.
SSR fetches /api/models, /api/blog on every request to localhost:8001. For high traffic, consider in-memory caching with TTL (e.g., 60s) to reduce backend load.

Two <h1> elements end up in the DOM (SSR seo-content + React rendered) — the SSR one is hidden via display:none after React mounts, which is correct for users but Lighthouse may still flag duplicate landmark; acceptable trade-off for SEO.
Inline <style> blocks inside SSR HTML use vendor colours hardcoded; OK for SEO snapshot, but consider extracting to a tiny SSR-only CSS file.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (SPA mount + admin login + admin/models + admin/contacts verified)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed data (Aurelia model, 8 services, 18 areas, blog posts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: SSR server is at /app/frontend/ssr-server.js + /app/frontend/ssr-data.js. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_ssr_routes.py` for raw-HTTP/no-JS verification. The literal string 'You need to enable JavaScript' WILL appear inside the SPA <noscript> fallback at the bottom of every page — that is expected and harmless; the test asserts that #seo-content with real <h1> content is present BEFORE that, which is what Googlebot indexes.
rca of the issue: Original bug: CRA dev server served the SPA shell index.html for all routes, so curl/Googlebot saw only the React placeholder. Fix: replaced `craco start` with a custom Node Express SSR server (ssr-server.js) that pre-renders HTML for every public route by fetching data from FastAPI and embedding it inside a #seo-content div, then loads the React bundle which hides the SSR layer on mount. Verified by GET (no JS) returning real <h1>/<title>/<meta>/JSON-LD for /, /models, /models/aurelia, /services, /services/*, /areas, /escort/*, /escort-hamburg, /blog, /blog/{slug}, /faq, /ueber-uns, /kontakt, /sitemap.xml. Admin routes intentionally fall through to the SPA shell.
